chunks.title: Inline, Internal & External CSS
chunks.text: In this video, we will see how to add CSS to an HTML page.

chunks.title: Inline, Internal & External CSS
chunks.text: There are three ways to add CSS to HTML.

chunks.title: Inline, Internal & External CSS
chunks.text: The first way is Inline CSS which is added to HTML pages using the style attribute.

chunks.title: Inline, Internal & External CSS
chunks.text: The second way is Internal CSS.

chunks.title: Inline, Internal & External CSS
chunks.text: Internal CSS means you add a style tag and put the CSS in the style tag of the HTML page.

chunks.title: Inline, Internal & External CSS
chunks.text: After that, the most used one is External CSS.

chunks.title: Inline, Internal & External CSS
chunks.text: It means you make a .css file and add all your styles in that .css file.

chunks.title: Inline, Internal & External CSS
chunks.text: I will tell you very closely in this video what is the difference between the three.

chunks.title: Inline, Internal & External CSS
chunks.text: When to use which and when to avoid which.
